title: Can a Web Designer Create a Mobile App?
excerpt: It's a fair question: if your web designer is talented, can they just handle your app design too? The answer depends on their skills and experience. This post helps you understand what's involved and how to evaluate whether a web designer is qualified to take on your app project.
content:
If you've already worked with a web designer and had great results, it makes sense to want to keep working with them for your mobile app. But is that a good idea?

The honest answer: it depends.

The Overlap Between Web and App Design

Many of the principles that make for great web design also apply to app design — visual hierarchy, color theory, typography, white space, and user-centered thinking all translate across platforms.

A skilled web designer who is curious, adaptable, and willing to learn platform-specific requirements can absolutely transition into app design. Many do.

What's Different About App Design

Where web designers often need to upskill for app projects:

  • Platform guidelines: iOS and Android have strict, specific design rules. A web designer unfamiliar with Apple's Human Interface Guidelines or Google's Material Design may inadvertently create interfaces that feel "off" — or that get rejected by the app store.
  • Touch interaction design: Designing for finger-based navigation requires different thinking about target sizes, gestures, and navigation patterns.
  • Native components: Apps use native UI components (switches, pickers, bottom sheets) that have no direct web equivalent. Knowing when and how to use these is important.
  • Performance considerations: App UI must feel responsive and fluid at 60fps. Design decisions directly affect this.

How to Evaluate Whether Your Web Designer Can Handle It

Ask them:

  • Have you designed mobile apps before? (See live examples.)

  • Are you familiar with Apple HIG and Material Design?

  • Do you design in device-specific frames and with mobile constraints in mind?

  • Have you worked with developers on native app builds?


When to Bring in a Specialist

For complex apps with deep native functionality, it's worth hiring a dedicated mobile UX/UI designer. The learning curve for a pure web designer can slow your project and introduce costly design errors.

The Safest Choice
